test_cases.prompt:
You are running bug-investigation. A user has reported the following bug. Investigate it and produce a BUGS.md entry with your findings.

## Bug Report
{fixture:1-clear-reproduction-steps}

## Project Context
This is a Python/Flask web application with PostgreSQL. The project is at /projects/webapp. The memory directory is /projects/webapp/hack. BUGS.md should be written to /projects/webapp/hack/BUGS.md.
expected_behaviors: Traces from symptom (login failure) to root cause (username string concatenation in SQL query at src/auth/password_check.py)
Produces BUGS.md entry with Status, Impact, Root Cause, Files Involved with line references
Includes Resolution Plan with actionable steps such as switching to parameterized query
Does NOT blame bcrypt.checkpw or the password field — password goes to bcrypt, not into SQL

test_cases.prompt:
You are running bug-investigation. A user has reported a vague performance issue. Investigate it and produce a BUGS.md entry with your findings.

## Bug Report
{fixture:2-vague-bug-report}

## Project Context
This is a Python/Flask web application with PostgreSQL, deployed on AWS ECS. The project is at /projects/webapp. The memory directory is /projects/webapp/hack. BUGS.md should be written to /projects/webapp/hack/BUGS.md.
expected_behaviors: Does NOT jump to a single cause without investigation — examines all potential hotspots
Examines the N+1 query pattern, the unbuffered file read, and the synchronous HTTP call
Identifies synchronous HTTP call to weather API as most likely cause based on evidence (15s timeout, external dependency, intermittent latency matches user report)
Produces structured BUGS.md entry with Investigating or Root Cause Found status

test_cases.prompt:
You are running bug-investigation. The QA team has reported three bugs simultaneously after a staging deployment. Handle all three and produce BUGS.md entries for each.

## Bug Reports
{fixture:3-multi-bug-batch}

## Project Context
This is a Python/Flask web application with PostgreSQL, deployed to staging. The project is at /projects/webapp. The memory directory is /projects/webapp/hack. BUGS.md should be written to /projects/webapp/hack/BUGS.md. Use sequential IDs starting from BUG-001.
expected_behaviors: Handles all 3 bugs as separate BUG entries with independent investigation
Dispatches or documents separate investigation per bug rather than combining them
Produces 3 distinct BUGS.md entries with independent Status, Impact, and Resolution Plans

test_cases.prompt:
Investigate this bug report and identify the root cause.

{fixture:4-dirty-app-race-condition}
expected_behaviors: Identifies TOCTOU race in permissions.py as the root cause: separate check and action queries allow race window
Explains why the issue is sporadic: depends on timing between concurrent requests
Does NOT blame the rate limiter for the permission issue — it is a separate bug
Examines the login.py session management as a potential alternative hypothesis but rules it out

test_cases.prompt:
Investigate this bug report and identify the root cause.

{fixture:5-dirty-app-subtle-ssrf}
expected_behaviors: Identifies SSRF as the root cause: URL validation only checks startswith('https://') without blocking internal IP ranges
Explains the security implication: internal network access through webhook URLs, not just a functional bug
Notes that the 'failing URLs' are all internal/private IP addresses (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x)
Does NOT just explain the functional failure — identifies the security surface
Does NOT conclude the bug is in permissions.py — the permission check is not responsible for the webhook URL validation failure
Investigates both Bug A (500 errors) and Bug B (deleted items in results) independently

Identifies that Bug A's 500 errors stem from the missing deleted_at IS NULL filter in search_tasks — soft-deleted tasks are returned by the ORM query and their to_dict() raises AttributeError when associated data is absent
Identifies that Bug B's root cause is the same missing soft-delete filter: search_tasks does not exclude rows where deleted_at IS NOT NULL
Recognizes that fixing the soft-delete filter (adding Task.deleted_at == None to the ORM query) resolves both Bug A and Bug B
Identifies the SQL injection in search_tasks_by_saved_term (line 41): f-string interpolates user_id and the search term directly into a raw SQL query with no parameterization

test_cases.prompt:
Investigate this bug report. The error appears in the tasks module but the root cause may be elsewhere.

{fixture:7-misdirected-symptom}
expected_behaviors: Traces the error from tasks/handlers.py (symptom) to auth/middleware.py (root cause) — middleware renamed g.current_user to g.auth_context in revision abc789
Does NOT conclude the bug is in tasks/handlers.py — the handler code is correct, the data it depends on was renamed upstream
Identifies ALL affected handlers, not just the reported one: tasks, projects, reports, and settings modules all read g.current_user
Notes that settings/handlers.py uses dict key access (g.current_user['id']) which raises KeyError, a different symptom from the same root cause
Proposes fix: update all handlers to use g.auth_context, OR revert the rename in middleware.py
